Add DG sections for Sort and Statistics features with sequence diagrams
Updated Expense constructor to automatically format all provided categories into Title Case. Added JUnit tests to verify lower-case and mixed-case inputs are standardized.
Added a check in Parser.parseAddCommand() to intercept /bin/zsh.00 inputs. Created a user-friendly warning message in Ui to explain why zero-value tracking is disabled.

commitResults.messageBody: Updated ExpenseList to track a master category list. Modified AddCommand to pause and display an interactive numbered prompt via Ui when the user omits the /c flag. Allows dynamic creation of new categories.

commitResults.messageTitle: Refactor SpendSwift loop to parser-command flow

commitResults.messageBody:
SpendSwift uses type checks to decide when to save data, which couples

persistence behavior to concrete command classes and makes extension harder.

Add a command-level shouldPersist() contract and use it in SpendSwift

to trigger storage saves after mutating commands.

Add Storage tests for save/load round-trip, malformed lines, and

invalid amount lines to verify the integration behavior.

commitResults.messageTitle: Harden parser, storage, and model defensiveness
commitResults.messageBody:
The integration flow needs stronger runtime safety at parser/model/storage

boundaries for invalid inputs and edge cases.

Add guard clauses and explicit IllegalArgumentException checks for

null/invalid values, validate delete index and add amount constraints,

and add Storage logging for load/save parse failures.

Enable assertions for Gradle run with -ea and extend tests to cover

new defensive behaviors and failure paths.
Add Java assert statements in AddCommand and HelpCommand

Replace the placeholder SpendSwift test with behavior-focused flow tests.

The new tests cover exit, add/list, and invalid command handling paths.
Refactor SpendSwift to parser-command flow
Add command-based storage persistence flow
Harden parser, storage, and model defensiveness
